decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly lower today with NASDAQ down 0.6% and S&P 500 down 0.3% — a neutral, low-conviction session with no clear catalyst for new positions. Cash sits at $477.14, just below the $500 threshold to trigger new buys, so the rules naturally keep us sidelined. The portfolio is up 31.6% overall and all six current positions remain intact — preserving those gains in a soft market is the right call.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities slipped modestly on Friday as investors digested mixed economic data and uncertainty around Federal Reserve rate policy heading into the end of Q2 2026. Tech-heavy NASDAQ led the decline at -0.6%, reflecting continued pressure on growth names amid elevated valuations. With no strong macro catalyst, trading was light and the session was characterized by profit-taking rather than any directional conviction.
market_education.summary_zh: 美股週五小幅下跌，投資者在第二季末消化混合經濟數據，並對聯準會利率政策走向保持觀望。以科技股為主的那斯達克指數跌幅較大，下挫0.6%，反映成長股在高估值環境下持續承壓。整體成交清淡，市場缺乏明確方向性催化劑，本輪下跌主要由獲利了結所驅動。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Dry powder refers to cash or liquid reserves kept on hand to deploy when the right opportunity arises. Today's decision not to buy is partly because our cash ($477) fell just below the $500 floor — a reminder that running too low on cash means you can't act when a high-conviction setup appears. Good investors always keep some dry powder so they're never forced to sit out a great trade.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「乾火藥」（Dry Powder）指投資者手中保留的現金或流動資金，以備在合適機會出現時迅速部署。今日我們未進行新買入，部分原因正是現金餘額（477美元）略低於500美元的入場門檻——這提醒我們，若現金消耗過度，一旦出現高確信度的買入機會便無法把握。優秀的投資者始終保留一定的乾火藥，確保在市場出現良機時能夠迅速行動。
